techniques.evidence_quote: The credentials were obtained from a popular target, a system running a vulnerable version of Laravel (CVE-2021-3129).
Once initial access was obtained, they exfiltrated cloud credentials
they intentionally set the max_tokens_to_sample parameter to -1 ... It confirmed not only the existence of access to the LLMs but also that these services were active
a broader script that was able to check credentials for ten different AI services in order to determine which were useful
logging settings are also queried where possible. This is done to avoid detection when using the compromised credentials to run their prompts.
the cost to the victim can be over $46,000 per day

attack_chain.description: Attackers exploited a vulnerable Laravel instance (CVE-2021-3129) to gain initial access to the target system.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: The credentials were obtained from a popular target, a system running a vulnerable version of Laravel (CVE-2021-3129).

attack_chain.description: Attackers exfiltrated cloud credentials stored on the compromised Laravel system.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: Once initial access was obtained, they exfiltrated cloud credentials and gained access to the cloud environment

evidence_chunks.quote: Once initial access was obtained, they exfiltrated cloud credentials and gained access to the cloud environment
Once initial access was obtained, they exfiltrated cloud credentials and gained access to the cloud environment

attack_chain.description: Attackers ran a key-checking script (keychecker) against the stolen credentials to enumerate which of ten AI/LLM services (AWS Bedrock, Anthropic, OpenAI, Azure, GCP Vertex, etc.) they provided access to.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: a broader script that was able to check credentials for ten different AI services in order to determine which were useful for their purposes

attack_chain.description: Attackers called Bedrock InvokeModel with max_tokens_to_sample=-1 to probe, via the ValidationException vs AccessDenied response, whether the stolen credentials had Bedrock model access enabled.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: they intentionally set the max_tokens_to_sample parameter to -1 ... It confirmed not only the existence of access to the LLMs but also that these services were active, as indicated by the resulting ValidationException

attack_chain.technique_name: Impair Defenses: Disable or Modify Cloud Logs
attack_chain.description: Attackers called GetModelInvocationLoggingConfiguration to determine whether Bedrock model invocation logging to CloudWatch/S3 was enabled, in order to avoid using credentials whose usage would be logged (OAI Reverse Proxy intentionally avoids keys with logging enabled).
attack_chain.evidence_quote: logging settings are also queried where possible. This is done to avoid detection when using the compromised credentials to run their prompts.

evidence_chunks.quote: logging settings are also queried where possible. This is done to avoid detection when using the compromised credentials to run their prompts.
logging settings are also queried where possible. This is done to avoid detection when using the compromised credentials to run their prompts.

attack_chain.description: Attackers abused access to hosted LLM models (Anthropic Claude v2/v3 via AWS Bedrock) through an OAI Reverse Proxy to sell LLM access to other cybercriminals, racking up potentially $46,000+/day in consumption costs for the victim (LLMjacking).
attack_chain.evidence_quote: attackers intend to sell LLM access to other cybercriminals while the cloud account owner pays the bill ... the cost to the victim can be over $46,000 per day

initial_access_vector: Exploitation of a publicly exposed Laravel application vulnerable to CVE-2021-3129, from which AWS cloud credentials were stolen.
persistence_mechanism: Continued use of stolen long-term IAM user access keys; no new persistence mechanisms were described.
privilege_escalation: None observed; attackers leveraged the existing permissions of the stolen IAM credentials (including Bedrock InvokeModel).
